title: Unknown XML Objects, Zod Admission, Loss-Aware Normalization, and Provenance · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs unknown xml objects, zod admission, loss-aware normalization, and provenance from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for runtime-validated normalized feed boundary after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Keep parser and database JSON values unknown until discriminated format, structural, scalar, cardinality, and semantic runtime validation passes.
Build distinct validated RSS and Atom transport variants and exhaustively normalize each supported case.
Select scoped stable identity from Atom ID, RSS GUID meaning, canonical link, or bounded fingerprint and report collisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Ts Normalization Lo…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 4964 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile unknown parser values, discriminated formats, identity, provenance, and normalization loss; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Return admitted, defaulted, dropped, conflicting, and unsupported fields with parser and policy version for each changed fixture.
